title: Macroplacis cordata
intro: Macroplacis cordata, also known as Caltrop, is a plant species belonging to the family Zygophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of M. cordata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Macroplacis cordata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat various ailments, and its roots are also used to make a tincture for external use.
taxonomy: Macroplacis cordata belongs to the family Menispermaceae. This classification places it among the menispermaceous plants, which are a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Macroplacis cordata is further categorized as a species within the genus Macroplacis. The family Menispermaceae is characterized by its unique seed structure and diverse distribution across various ecosystems. Macroplacis cordata is also sometimes referred to as a member of the order Ranunculales.
